paper_title: From Products to Capabilities: Constructing a Genotypic Product Space
research_answer: More common than one might expect from the structural barriers they face. Among countries that had fewer than 20 export industries in 1992–1996, 30% managed to add at least 5 new industries and 50% improved on their baseline diversification level within a 5-year window. This suggests that while low-diversification countries face real structural constraints — especially those below the ~270-capability threshold — export diversification is not simply locked in. A meaningful share of less-diversified countries do make measurable progress. The challenge is that Vietnam-style transformations, which require acquiring ~40 new capabilities and entering a dozen new industries, are the more demanding end of the spectrum.
supporting_quotes: Among the set of countries with less than 20 industries in 1992 to 1996 in our sample, 30% diversified their export by at least 5 industries and 50% compared to their baseline diversification.
